name.text: Finding area of a kite given diagonals

questiontext.text:
A kite has diagonals measuring {#d1#} {#unit#} and {#d2#} cm.

Find its area.

[[jsxgraph width="300px" height="400px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, {boundingbox: [-10, 15, 10, -15], axis: false, showCopyright: false}); // Define the vertices of the kite based on diagonals // Vertical diagonal (d1) and Horizontal diagonal (d2) var top = board.create('point', [0, {#d1/2#}], {visible: false}); var bottom = board.create('point', [0, -{#d1/2#}], {visible: false}); var left = board.create('point', [-{#d2/2#}, 0], {visible: false}); var right = board.create('point', [{#d2/2#}, 0], {visible: false}); // Draw the Kite board.create('polygon', [top, right, bottom, left], { fillColor: 'none', strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2 }); // Draw the dashed diagonal lines board.create('segment', [top, bottom], {dash: 2, strokeColor: 'gray'}); board.create('segment', [left, right], {dash: 2, strokeColor: 'gray'}); // Labels for the diagonals board.create('text', [0.5, 0, '{#d1#} cm'], {fontSize: 14}); board.create('text', [-1, -1, '{#d2#} cm'], {fontSize: 14}); [[/jsxgraph]]

Area = [[input:ans1]] cm\(^2\) [[validation:ans1]] [[feedback:prt1]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

To find the area of a kite, we use the formula involving the lengths of its diagonals \(d_1\) and \(d_2\):

\[ \text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times d_1 \times d_2 \]

Substituting the given values:

\[ \text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times {@d1@} \times {@d2@} \] \[ \text{Area} = {@ta@} \text{ {#unit#}}^2 \]

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
